articles.title: Google Releases Nano Banana 2 Lite, its Fastest and Cheapest AI Image Generator Yet
articles.summary: Google's Nano Banana 2 Lite is an image model that can generate visuals in four seconds for under four cents per thousand. It is now available in AI Studio, the Gemini API, and the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. Built for speed over fidelity, it replaces the original "legacy" model while retaining prompt adherence, character consistency, and text rendering. Nano Banana 2 Lite is rolling out across consumer products like Search, Gemini, and Photos. Alongside it, Gemini Omni Flash video generation is now open to developers at ten cents per second. The model is a companion pipeline that turns generated images into short clips.

articles.title: Koto redesigns Stack Overflow to champion human knowledge in the AI era
articles.summary: Koto has rebranded Stack Overflow around the idea that trustworthy, human-validated knowledge is the foundation of effective AI, repositioning the platform from a Q&A site to an essential resource for developers and enterprises. The new identity centers on the concept of “Always in build,” introducing an evolved logo, a stack-based visual system, the custom Stack Sans typeface, AI-assisted design tools, and a clearer tone of voice that reflects the collaborative, iterative nature of the developer community. Developed with community feedback and refined after its 2025 debut, the rebrand aims to reinforce Stack Overflow's role as a living source of reliable technical knowledge in the AI era.

articles.title: watchOS 27 has brand new default Home Screen for Apple Watch
articles.summary: watchOS 27 introduces the Dynamic app grid as the new default Apple Watch Home Screen, placing the Siri app at the centre and surrounding it with AI-powered suggestions based on your most-used and recently used apps. The traditional Grid and List views remain available but are now accessed through a shortcut at the bottom of the dynamic grid. Apple aims to make app access faster and more relevant, with early beta testing suggesting the recommendations work well.

articles.title: Finding photos is so much easier with Siri AI in iOS 27 that I no longer scroll
articles.summary: iOS 27's Siri AI improves photo search by letting users find images with natural-language voice commands, making it much easier to locate specific photos in large libraries without endless scrolling. It generally performs well when searching for objects, people, or clothing, and allows users to quickly share results or open them in the Photos app, though it can still make mistakes with more complex or time-based queries. Despite some inaccuracies in the beta, the feature is a significant improvement over previous photo search capabilities and saves considerable time.

articles.title: You're missing the point; this is your real value in tech companies
articles.summary: AI is most valuable when it complements human expertise rather than replaces it, as businesses that rely solely on automation risk creating generic products while losing the strategic, creative, and problem-solving strengths people provide. A comparison between AI-only, human-only, and hybrid approaches shows that the strongest outcomes come from combining AI's speed and scalability with human judgment, originality, and vision. As AI reshapes industries, the most valuable skills are no longer just knowing how to use the latest tools, but applying critical thinking, strategy, and creativity to solve meaningful problems.

articles.title: The state of the creative industry 2026: what our survey tells us about pay, burnout and AI
articles.summary: Creative Boom's 2026 survey of 882 creative professionals reveals an industry under significant strain, with 69% reporting burnout, widespread financial insecurity, and 86% using AI despite only 10% believing it has a positive impact. Many creatives feel pressured to adopt AI while worrying about lower fees, increased competition, and the future of the profession, with nearly half of freelancers earning under £30,000 a year and 38% considering changing jobs. Rather than seeking more technology, respondents identified stronger professional communities, networking, and mentorship as the most valuable ways to improve their careers and wellbeing.

articles.title: v0 Template Gallery (Website)
articles.summary: Discover the best apps, components, and starters from the v0 community.

articles.title: Create Viral Ads by Chatting (Website)
articles.summary: Turn an idea into ready-to-run automated video ads for TikTok, Meta, and YouTube with the ultimate AI ad generator.

articles.title: 39 principles for designing human-AI interaction
articles.summary: Designing AI products requires rethinking traditional UI principles because AI is probabilistic rather than deterministic, making user trust, transparency, control, and recoverability as important as model capability. The framework presents 39 design principles covering areas such as setting clear expectations, showing evidence instead of confidence scores, keeping users in control, making AI outputs editable and verifiable, designing for graceful failure, and limiting autonomy based on risk. The overarching goal is to help users rely on AI appropriately by treating it as a collaborative tool whose decisions are understandable, inspectable, and always subject to human oversight.

articles.title: The Future of UX in an AI World: Why Designers are Becoming Strategic Leaders
articles.summary: AI is reshaping digital products, pushing UX designers beyond interface work into strategic decisions about trust, autonomy, and how humans and AI should interact. As uncertainty grows around AI-powered features, user research becomes essential for uncovering how people actually behave, rather than relying on assumptions. This shift is opening new career paths, with designers gaining greater influence over product direction, roadmap planning, and cross-functional decision-making.

articles.title: Designed for a Dead Language
articles.summary: Modern language-learning apps like Duolingo and Anki inherited their grammar-drill structure from the Prussian Grammar-Translation method, originally designed in 1788 to teach Latin at scale. That method conflated literacy with fluency, prioritizing gradable metrics over actual communication, a flaw later articulated by linguist Stephen Krashen's distinction between acquisition and learning. Newer tools like Praktika, Langua, and Rosetta Stone instead remove access barriers to immersive, conversation-based learning, which research suggests produces stronger speaking results.

articles.title: Why are We All Obsessed with Anti-design?
articles.summary: Anti-design has resurged over the past decade as a reaction to minimalism's polished, white-space-heavy aesthetic that grew tiresome to audiences.

articles.title: Creativity Lessons from The Eiffel Tower
articles.summary: The Eiffel Tower offers four creativity lessons drawn from a book on creative thinking, starting with how its design built on earlier works and multiple collaborators rather than solitary genius.
